description: The WRONG query form for compound-surname parentage: the adjacent phrase `+"Naveda Somarriba"`. Because the two surnames belong to the father and mother separately in the parents' own parish acts, they are never adjacent there — so the phrase matches only records where the child's OWN compound name is written out contiguously. Returns a single Mexican record (Francisco as a 1877 baptism godfather) that does NOT name his parents, so it fails to answer the question. Serves to penalize the phrase form relative to the co-occurrence fixture.

projectPath.description: Absolute path to the active project directory. Supply it whenever the search will be logged (the normal case): the tool then stages its raw results host-side and returns a `staged.resultsRef` handle to pass to research_log_append as stagedResultsRef, so the results are retained without re-serializing them. Only omit it for a throwaway exploratory search you are certain you will not log.

results.collectionTitle: México, Veracruz, registros parroquiales y diocesanos, 1590-1978
results.title: Santa Maria, Papantla, Veracruz Baptism
results.textDocument: En la Villa de Papantla a dos de Abril de mil ochocientos setenta y siete ... fueron padrinos Francisco Naveda Somarriba y Cándida García, a quienes advertí el parentesco espiritual.
